req.title: Manager creates performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a performance-review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) an authenticated manager can create a performance-review via POST /api/performance-reviews and receive 200/201 with an entity id; (2) mandatory field validation returns 400; (3) unauthenticated requests return 401; (4) forbidden roles return 403; (5) an audit record is written on success. I apply EP to cover the valid-input partition and the missing-field partition, BVA to probe the maximum allowed length of a string field (review_text), NEG for authentication/authorisation failures, DT for the multi-condition auth+role decision, and STC for the state change from 'no review' to 'review created + audit written'. Six tests cover positive, negative, and boundary kinds as required.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_performance_review_success
preconditions: a valid manager auth token is available via <manager_token fixture>
a valid employee_id is available via <employee_id fixture>
actions: POST /api/performance-reviews with all mandatory fields and valid manager bearer token
expected: response body contains a non-empty 'id' field
GET /api/audit-records?entity_id=<id> returns at least one audit record for the created review
trace: R-WEB-021: positive outcome – review created, audit written, entity id returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_manager_creates_performance_review_success(manager_token, employee_id):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": employee_id,
        "period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 4,
        "review_text": "Meets all expectations."
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    review_id = body.get("id")
    assert review_id, "Response must contain a non-empty 'id'"
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/audit-records",
        params={"entity_id": review_id},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    records = audit_resp.json()
    assert len(records) >= 1, "At least one audit record must exist for the created review"

tests.name: test_create_performance_review_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
preconditions: a valid manager auth token is available via <manager_token fixture>
actions: POST /api/performance-reviews with an empty JSON body and valid manager bearer token
expected: response body contains a 'errors' or 'message' field describing validation failures
trace: R-WEB-021: error pathway – 400 on validation error when mandatory fields are absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_create_performance_review_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(manager_token):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/performance-reviews", json={}, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    has_error_info = "errors" in body or "message" in body
    assert has_error_info, "Response must contain validation error details"

tests.name: test_create_performance_review_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /api/performance-reviews with all mandatory fields but no Authorization header
trace: R-WEB-021: error pathway – 401 if unauthenticated (DT rule: auth=false, role=any → 401)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_create_performance_review_unauthenticated_returns_401():
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        "period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 3,
        "review_text": "Sample review."
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/performance-reviews", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_create_performance_review_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: a valid auth token for a non-manager role (e.g. employee) is available via <employee_token fixture>
actions: POST /api/performance-reviews with all mandatory fields using an employee bearer token
trace: R-WEB-021: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden (DT rule: auth=true, role!=manager → 403)

tests.name: test_create_performance_review_review_text_at_max_length
preconditions: a valid manager auth token is available via <manager_token fixture>
a valid employee_id is available via <employee_id fixture>
the API documentation states review_text max length is 2000 characters
actions: POST /api/performance-reviews with review_text exactly 2000 characters long and all other mandatory fields valid
expected: response body contains a non-empty 'id' field
trace: R-WEB-021: boundary – review_text at upper boundary (2000 chars) must be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
MAX_REVIEW_TEXT_LENGTH = 2000

def test_create_performance_review_review_text_at_max_length(manager_token, employee_id):
    review_text = "A" * MAX_REVIEW_TEXT_LENGTH
    assert len(review_text) == MAX_REVIEW_TEXT_LENGTH
    payload = {
        "employee_id": employee_id,
        "period": "2024-Q2",
        "rating": 5,
        "review_text": review_text
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max length, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id"), "Response must contain a non-empty 'id' at boundary length"

tests.name: test_create_performance_review_review_text_exceeds_max_length
preconditions: a valid manager auth token is available via <manager_token fixture>
a valid employee_id is available via <employee_id fixture>
the API documentation states review_text max length is 2000 characters
actions: POST /api/performance-reviews with review_text exactly 2001 characters long and all other mandatory fields valid
expected: response body indicates review_text length validation failure
trace: R-WEB-021: boundary – review_text one character beyond upper boundary (2001 chars) must be rejected with 400
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
MAX_REVIEW_TEXT_LENGTH = 2000

def test_create_performance_review_review_text_exceeds_max_length(manager_token, employee_id):
    review_text = "A" * (MAX_REVIEW_TEXT_LENGTH + 1)
    assert len(review_text) == MAX_REVIEW_TEXT_LENGTH + 1
    payload = {
        "employee_id": employee_id,
        "period": "2024-Q2",
        "rating": 5,
        "review_text": review_text
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 beyond max length, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    has_error_info = "errors" in body or "message" in body
    assert has_error_info, "Response must contain validation error details for review_text"
